executive_summary: We continue to dominate illicit finance attribution and sanctions enforcement narratives, with high-profile coverage on THORChain hacker tracing and Iran's crypto infrastructure. Simultaneously, the competitive landscape intensified sharply as Elliptic secured $120M in institutional backing, while AI-powered compliance automation emerges as the defining competitive battleground across our sector and adjacent markets.

market_landscape.title: Institutional Capital Flood Into Crypto Infrastructure
market_landscape.body: Elliptic's $120M raise and JPMorgan's tokenized fund filings signal Wall Street is now betting materially on crypto-native and tokenization infrastructure. This capital concentration creates both opportunity (expanded compliance demand) and risk (well-funded competitors in our core markets).

market_landscape.title: AI-Driven Compliance as Existential Competitive Threshold
market_landscape.body: Elliptic's messaging on AI overwhelming compliance teams and broader Bloomberg coverage of AI-powered financial oversight make automation capability table stakes for any analytics vendor. We must accelerate AI narrative and product differentiation or risk commoditization of our core analytics offerings.

market_landscape.title: Regulatory Tailwinds Enable Market Maturation
market_landscape.body: CLARITY Act momentum, Bitcoin's sustained $80K+ pricing, and Bloomberg's serious institutional coverage signal regulatory clarity is finally arriving. This legitimacy expands TAM significantly but also brings TradFi compliance frameworks that our institutional customers will demand we support natively.

competitor_reads.Elliptic: Major institutional funding win ($120M from Nasdaq, Deutsche Bank) signals strong TradFi momentum and positions them as AI-first compliance player; CEO's public stance on AI overwhelm compliance teams shows aggressive thought leadership positioning that we must counter with our own automation narrative.
competitor_reads.TRM Labs: Strategic partnerships with Tether and TRON through T3 Financial Crime Unit demonstrate ecosystem integration and law enforcement traction ($450M frozen assets); positioning as enforcement-outcome focused rather than pure analytics, complementary to our attribution focus but gaining regulatory credibility.
competitor_reads.Arkham Intelligence: Continues expansion into on-chain data intelligence (exchange decentralization pivot, token holder tracking) but limited compliance/enforcement narrative coverage; positioning as retail/institutional data access layer rather than compliance infrastructure, reducing direct competitive overlap this period.
competitor_reads.Crystal Intelligence: Limited coverage this period (1 article on North Korea $7.1B tracing); signals lower media velocity compared to peers, suggesting resource constraints or narrower geographic/threat focus relative to our multi-domain presence.
